titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Annie Allender Gould Papers
titlestmt.author: Tina Evans

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 5, 2024 at 03:20

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Auger Down Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in six series: I: Correspondence, 1858 - 1927. II: Diaries, 1874 - 1891. III: Education, 1830 - 1917. IV: Missionary work, 1879 - 1926. V: Photographs, 1849-02 - 1922. VI: Printed material, 1872 - 1895.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Annie Allender Gould was born in 1867, in Portland, Maine. She attended Portland public schools and in 1887, she entered Mount Holyoke Seminary, graduating in 1892.

In August 1893 she left Maine and went to Pao Ting Fu, China as a Presbyterian missionary from the American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions. She remain in China doing missionary work until 1900 when she was killed during the Boxer Rebellion.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Annie Allender Gould Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence, writings, printed material, photographs, and other papers by or relating to Annie Allender Gould, the Gould family, and Gould's missionary work in China during the Boxer Rebellion.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Annie Allender Gould Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily contains correspondence to and from Annie Allender Gould and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence between Annie Allender Gould and the Gould family, as well as correspondence to the Gould family from others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters to Annie Allender Gould during her time at Mount Holyoke College.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notebook in the hand of John Mead Gould that lists letters received from Annie Allender Gould with additional correspondence laid in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains postcard written in mirror writing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains diaries kept by Annie Allender Gould, with some portions written by her father, John Mead Gould.

did.unittitle: Annie Allender Gould and John Mead Gould

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portions of the diary are in the hand of John Mead Gould.

did.unittitle: Report cards from Cumberland Street Grammar School, Portland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains dance card, programs, broadsides, and other printed items.

did.unittitle: Catalogue. Memorandum Society, and Alumnae at Mount Holyoke Seminary, 1877-1887
Mount Holyoke Female Seminary, South Hadley Mass

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three copies.

did.unittitle: Third annual report of the College Settlements Association from September 1, 1891 to September 1, 1892

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to Annie Allender Gould's work as a Presbyterian missionary including clippings, ephemera, photographs, and other related documentation. Posthumous tributes created after Allender Gould's death in the Boxer Rebellion are also included.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are primarily arranged chronologically, with some undated materials maintaining order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains programs, clipping, and certificates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains issues of the Ocean Pebbles Mission periodical.

did.unittitle: Ocean Pebbles Mission Band account book
Ocean Pebbles Mission Band financial papers
Ocean Pebbles Mission Circle meeting minutes
Young Ladies Mission Band financial papers
American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions questionnaire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains printed questionnaire, completed in manuscript by Annie Allender Gould.

did.unittitle: Program for Mary S. Morrill farewell missionary service
R. M. S. Empress of Japan concert program
Tribute from American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four copies of reprinted tribute.

did.unittitle: Chase-Chace family association second and third reunions
Circular documenting claims against foreign governments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two copies of tribute.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper with account of Annie Allender Gould's death, which occurred during the Boxer Rebellion.

did.unittitle: Gertrude H. Blanchard postcards to John Mead Gould
Laura Jones letter to John Mead Gould
Ella J. Vinton letter to Laura Jones

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs of Annie Allender Gould, her family, friends, and other individuals. Some titles are derived from the manuscript annotations on the photographs.

did.unittitle: Photographs with manuscript descriptions of #3 Miss Reed's letters

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
Gould, Annie Allender, Mary Morrill, and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photograph of the children of Edward and Althea Gould: William Edward, John Mead, Elizabeth, Howard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photograph of John Mead and and Amelia J. Gould, Annie Allender Gould, Oliver C. Gould, and Theodore Gould.

did.unittitle: Charles Phelps William Merrit and Anna Celina Lord
Unidentified friends of Annie Allender Gould

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains printed material including programs and writings by Gould family members.

did.unittitle: Benjamin Apthorp Gould: The ancestry and posterity of Zaccheus Gould of Topsfield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains printed version with manuscript notes tipped in, as well as manuscript and typescript notes laid in.

did.unittitle: John Mead Gould: Gen. Mansfield at Antietam
